name: Potential Homoglyph Usage (Flows)
notes:
This rule triggers when a domain name contains a homoglyph character(s), which could make a domain appear the same as a trusted domain and redirects to a malicious host.
The custom function HOMOGLYPH::DETECTED, which is pre-filled with 1792 entries of homoglyph characters, accepts a string and returns true if the string contains a homoglyph character.

Note: Tune the rule with valid characters before enabling it. To view the flows that would trigger this rule, use the search "Potential Homoglyph Usage (Flows)".
conditions_text: when the flow context is Local to Local
when the flow matches HTTP Host is not N/A
when the flow matches HOMOGLYPH::DETECTED("HTTP Host") == TRUE AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Potential Homoglyph Usage (Flows) on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND NOT when the flow context is Local to Local
AND when the flow matches HTTP Host is not N/A
AND when the flow matches HOMOGLYPH::DETECTED("HTTP Host") == TRUE AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: A flow with a domain name containing a homoglyph character has been detected.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
